title: L004: Add network chaos scenarios and CI integration
summary: Review of changes addressing sourcery-ai[bot] feedback on PR #220. Two items were addressed: (1) the restart-count validation script in the network-latency chainsaw test was refactored from single-item extraction (items[0]) to iterating all matched operator pods, preventing scaled deployments from hiding partial instability; (2) the e2e-chaos CI workflow condition was guarded with github.event_name == 'pull_request' before accessing github.event.pull_request.labels, avoiding reliance on implicit null handling on push events. Both fixes are minimal, correct, and well-commented. Review pattern files and external review record were updated accordingly.

tests_checklist.item: E2E chaos tests exist for network-latency scenario (chainsaw-test.yaml)
Restart-count validation covers all operator pods, not just first
Guard ensures at least one pod exists before iterating (no vacuous pass)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing chaos E2E test patterns (6-step structure, catch blocks, diagnostics.sh)
CI workflow condition follows established pattern with proper boolean guards
Changes are minimal and scoped to exactly what the external review requested
No dead code or unnecessary additions

security_checklist.item: No shell injection vectors in script content
CI workflow uses SHA-pinned action references (existing, unchanged)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is the simplest possible fix for both review items
No scope creep beyond the two external review items

fail_fast_checklist.item: Pod existence guard fails early with descriptive error before restart-count loop
FAILED flag accumulates all pod failures before exiting (no silent partial pass)

defensive_checklist.item: RESTARTS variable defaults to 0 via ${RESTARTS:-0} if jsonpath returns empty
event_name guard prevents null property access on push events

suggested_improvements: The CC-0049 entry in check-new-resources-for-all-standard-fields-present-in-siblings.md is about event-type field guarding, not about sibling resource field parity. Consider creating a dedicated review pattern (e.g., 'guard-event-type-specific-fields-in-cross-event-workflows') for better discoverability. Low priority since the content itself is correct.

The CC-0049 entry in validate-implicit-defaults-not-just-explicit-fields.md is about single-item extraction from lists, not about implicit defaults. Consider creating a dedicated pattern (e.g., 'iterate-all-matched-resources-not-just-first-item') for better categorization. Low priority since the content itself is correct.
